---
title: "Adding an Agent to Vatican Explorer"
subtitle: "From SQL pipelines to natural-language exploration"
author: "Yangyang Li"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: serif
    slide-level: 2
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: github
    incremental: false
    footer: "Vatican Explorer · agent proposal"
    mermaid:
      theme: neutral
---

```{=html}
<style>
  :root {
    --vat-red:   #7b1e2b;
    --vat-red-2: #9b2a3a;
    --vat-gold:  #c9a227;
    --vat-cream: #fdf6e3;
    --vat-cream-2: #f3e7c4;
    --vat-ink:   #2b2b2b;
    --vat-dim:   #6b6b6b;
  }

  /* Headings */
  .reveal h1,
  .reveal h2,
  .reveal h3 {
    color: var(--vat-red);
    font-family: Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  }
  .reveal h2 {
    border-bottom: 2px solid var(--vat-gold);
    padding-bottom: 0.25em;
    display: inline-block;
  }

  /* Code blocks: cream background, red left rule */
  .reveal pre {
    border-left: 4px solid var(--vat-red);
    background: var(--vat-cream);
    border-radius: 6px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
  }
  .reveal code {
    color: var(--vat-red-2);
  }
  .reveal pre code {
    color: var(--vat-ink);
  }

  /* Tables: zebra stripes + red header */
  .reveal table {
    border-collapse: collapse;
    margin: 0.5em auto;
    font-size: 0.78em;
  }
  .reveal table th {
    background: var(--vat-red);
    color: var(--vat-cream);
    padding: 0.4em 0.7em;
    text-align: left;
  }
  .reveal table td {
    padding: 0.35em 0.7em;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--vat-cream-2);
  }
  .reveal table tr:nth-child(even) td {
    background: var(--vat-cream);
  }

  /* Tool catalog: responsive card grid */
  .tool-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
    gap: 0.8rem;
    margin-top: 0.6em;
  }
  @media (max-width: 900px) {
    .tool-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
  }
  .tool-card {
    background: var(--vat-cream);
    border: 1px solid var(--vat-red);
    border-top: 3px solid var(--vat-gold);
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 0.6rem 0.8rem;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
    font-size: 0.72em;
    text-align: left;
  }
  .tool-card h3 {
    margin: 0 0 0.3em 0;
    font-family: "Fira Code", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    color: var(--vat-red);
    font-size: 1em;
    border: none;
  }
  .tool-card .tool-name {
    font-family: "Fira Code", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    color: var(--vat-red);
    font-size: 1em;
    font-weight: 700;
    margin-bottom: 0.35em;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--vat-cream-2);
    padding-bottom: 0.25em;
  }
  .tool-card .tool-desc {
    margin: 0.2em 0 0.5em 0;
    color: var(--vat-ink);
    line-height: 1.35;
  }
  .tool-card p {
    margin: 0.2em 0 0.5em 0;
    color: var(--vat-ink);
    line-height: 1.35;
  }
  .tool-card .wraps {
    font-family: "Fira Code", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    font-size: 0.82em;
    color: var(--vat-dim);
    border-top: 1px dashed var(--vat-cream-2);
    padding-top: 0.35em;
    margin-top: 0.35em;
    word-break: break-word;
  }

  /* Fake terminal for the scripted demo */
  .terminal {
    background: #1e1e1e;
    color: #e6e6e6;
    border-radius: 10px;
    padding: 2.2em 1.1em 1.1em 1.1em;
    position: relative;
    margin: 0.6em auto;
    box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.35);
    font-family: "Fira Code", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    font-size: 0.62em;
    text-align: left;
    max-width: 92%;
  }
  .terminal::before {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    top: 0.7em;
    left: 0.9em;
    width: 12px;
    height: 12px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    background: #ff5f56;
    box-shadow:
      18px 0 0 #ffbd2e,
      36px 0 0 #27c93f;
  }
  .terminal::after {
    content: "agent session";
    position: absolute;
    top: 0.55em;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    text-align: center;
    color: #888;
    font-size: 0.95em;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  }
  .terminal div.sourceCode,
  .terminal .sourceCode {
    background: transparent !important;
    border: none !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
    margin: 0 !important;
    padding: 0 !important;
  }
  .terminal pre,
  .terminal pre.sourceCode {
    background: transparent !important;
    border: none !important;
    border-left: none !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
    padding: 0 !important;
    margin: 0 !important;
    color: #e6e6e6 !important;
  }
  .terminal pre code,
  .terminal pre.sourceCode code,
  .terminal code.sourceCode {
    color: #e6e6e6 !important;
    background: transparent !important;
  }
  /* Force every syntax-highlighted token inside the terminal to inherit */
  .terminal pre code span,
  .terminal pre.sourceCode code span,
  .terminal pre code *,
  .terminal .sourceCode span {
    color: #e6e6e6 !important;
    background: transparent !important;
  }

  /* Section dividers */
  .section-divider {
    text-align: center;
  }
  .section-divider .sec-num {
    display: block;
    font-family: Georgia, serif;
    font-size: 5em;
    color: var(--vat-gold);
    line-height: 1;
    margin-bottom: 0.1em;
    letter-spacing: 4px;
  }
  .section-divider .sec-title {
    display: block;
    font-family: Georgia, serif;
    font-size: 2.2em;
    color: var(--vat-red);
    border-top: 2px solid var(--vat-gold);
    border-bottom: 2px solid var(--vat-gold);
    padding: 0.3em 0;
    margin: 0 auto;
    max-width: 70%;
  }

  /* Two-column compare cards for "agent vs chatbot" */
  .compare-col {
    background: var(--vat-cream);
    border: 1px solid var(--vat-cream-2);
    border-top: 3px solid var(--vat-red);
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 0.8em 1em;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
  }
  .compare-col h4 {
    margin-top: 0;
    color: var(--vat-red);
    font-family: Georgia, serif;
  }
  .compare-col .compare-title {
    margin: 0 0 0.5em 0;
    color: var(--vat-red);
    font-family: Georgia, serif;
    font-weight: 700;
    font-size: 1.25em;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--vat-gold);
    padding-bottom: 0.2em;
  }

  /* Title-slide flourishes */
  .reveal .slides > section:first-child h1 {
    border-bottom: 3px solid var(--vat-gold);
    padding-bottom: 0.2em;
    display: inline-block;
  }

  /* Footer tint */
  .reveal .footer {
    color: var(--vat-dim);
    font-style: italic;
  }

  /* Small-text helper */
  .tight li { margin: 0.2em 0; }
  .muted { color: var(--vat-dim); font-size: 0.85em; }
</style>
```
---

::: {.section-divider}
[01]{.sec-num}
[Where we are]{.sec-title}
:::

---

## Where we are today

**Vatican Explorer**, DSSV FY26 Data Conclave project.

- Scrapes speeches, homilies, and audiences from `vatican.va`
- Stores them in a local SQLite database
- Enables analyses like *"who cites 1 John 4 and how often?"*

Working branch: `agent`.
Goal of this talk: **propose** giving the corpus a natural-language front door, and decide together if it's worth building.

---

## What we already built

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'primaryColor':'#fdf6e3','primaryTextColor':'#2b2b2b','primaryBorderColor':'#7b1e2b','lineColor':'#c9a227','fontFamily':'Georgia, serif'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A([vatican.va]) --> B[scraper<br/>6 steps]
  B --> C[(SQLite<br/>vatican_texts.db)]
  C --> D[database_helpers]
  D --> E[search<br/>biblical citations]
  D --> F[data_cleaning]
  D --> G[notebooks<br/>analyses/]
```

Key files:

- `vatican_scraper/step06_run_scraping_pipeline.py`
- `database_utils/database_helpers.py`
- `search/search_biblical_citation.py`

---

## Our SQL backbone

```sql
CREATE TABLE popes (
  _pope_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  pope_name TEXT, pope_number TEXT,
  secular_name TEXT, place_of_birth TEXT,
  pontificate_begin TEXT, pontificate_end TEXT,
  UNIQUE(pope_name, pope_number)
);

CREATE TABLE texts (
  _texts_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  pope_id INTEGER, section TEXT, year TEXT,
  date TEXT, location TEXT, title TEXT,
  language TEXT, url TEXT, text_content TEXT,
  FOREIGN KEY (pope_id) REFERENCES popes(_pope_id)
);
```


---

## The friction today

To ask: *"How did Benedict XVI talk about mercy in 2010?"*
a user must:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
```python
conn, cur = connect_to_database()
register_regexp_function(conn)
cur.execute("""
  SELECT t.title, t.date, t.text_content
  FROM texts t JOIN popes p
  ON t.pope_id = p._pope_id
  WHERE p.pope_name = ?
    AND t.year = ?
    AND t.text_content REGEXP ?
""", ("Benedict XVI", "2010", r"mercy|misericord"))
```
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
::: {.callout-warning}
**Requires knowing**

- The schema
- Python + SQL
- Regex
- Which notebook to open
:::
:::
:::

[Not accessible to historians or theologians.]{.muted}

---

::: {.section-divider}
[02]{.sec-num}
[Why an agent?]{.sec-title}
:::

---

## The proposal

> Same backend. **New front door.**

::: {.incremental}
- User asks a question in plain English.
- An **agent** would pick the right queries, run them against our existing DB, and return a grounded answer with citations.
- No new scraping, no new schema; we reuse what we already built.
- **Today is about deciding if this is worth doing**, not about a finished product.
:::

---

## What is an agent?

An **agent** is an LLM *in a loop* that can **call tools**.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}
<div class="compare-col">
<p class="compare-title">Chatbot</p>

- One shot
- Text in, text out
- Cannot touch your data
- Can make things up

</div>
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
<div class="compare-col">
<p class="compare-title">Agent</p>

- Runs in a loop
- Decides which tool to call
- Reads real results
- Iterates until done

</div>
:::
:::

The LLM is the planner. **Your functions are the hands.**

---

## The agent loop

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'primaryColor':'#fdf6e3','primaryTextColor':'#2b2b2b','primaryBorderColor':'#7b1e2b','lineColor':'#c9a227','fontFamily':'Georgia, serif'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  U([User<br/>question]) --> L[LLM<br/>plan]
  L -->|tool_call| T[Run tool<br/>SQL / regex / ...]
  T -->|observation| L
  L -->|done| A([Final answer<br/>with citations])
```

[Every hop is transparent: we can log it, test it, and debug it.]{.muted}

---

## Tools are just functions

We already wrote most of them.

```python
# database_helpers.py (existing)
def fetch_rows_by_regexp(cursor, query, pattern): ...

# search_biblical_citation.py (existing)
def search_biblical_citations_db(pattern, query): ...
```

Turn them into agent tools with a Pydantic schema:

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class SearchTextsInput(BaseModel):
    pope:    str | None = Field(None, description="e.g. 'Francis'")
    section: str | None = Field(None, description="homilies | audiences | ...")
    year:    str | None = None
    regex:   str | None = Field(None, description="Python regex over text_content")
```

**We have already written 90% of the agent.** The LLM just picks which one to run.

---

::: {.section-divider}
[03]{.sec-num}
[The tech]{.sec-title}
:::

---

## Tech stack: the choices

| Layer           | Options                                                 |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| LLM provider    | Anthropic Claude · OpenAI · Ollama (local)              |
| Agent framework | Raw SDK loop · **PydanticAI** · LangChain · LlamaIndex  |
| Validation      | **Pydantic**                                            |
| Vector store    | (optional) sqlite-vec · Chroma                          |
| CLI             | `python -m dc26_vatican_explorer.agent`                 |

[Evaluation axes: stability, lock-in, typed I/O, deps, offline story.]{.muted}

---

## Tech stack: our pick

::: {.callout-tip}
**Anthropic Claude + PydanticAI + Pydantic**
:::

Why:

- First-class **tool use** that is stable and well-documented
- **Typed tool I/O** matches our existing function signatures naturally
- **Minimal new dependencies**, no heavy framework lock-in
- Fits a clean `pyproject.toml` optional group:

```toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
agent = ["anthropic", "pydantic-ai", "pydantic>=2"]
```

Fallbacks if constraints change:

- **OpenAI + raw SDK.** If we need OpenAI-specific features.
- **Ollama + local model.** If we go fully offline or zero-cost.

---

## How we *could* build it

Proposed layout (parallel to `search/`, `pope_comparison/`):

```
src/dc26_vatican_explorer/agent/
  __init__.py
  tools.py      # @tool wrappers around DB helpers
  schemas.py    # Pydantic models for tool I/O
  runner.py     # the LLM loop
  __main__.py   # python -m ...agent
```

---

## Tool catalog (v1)

```{=html}
<div class="tool-grid">

  <div class="tool-card">
    <div class="tool-name"><code>list_popes()</code></div>
    <div class="tool-desc">Return every pope with metadata.</div>
    <div class="wraps">SELECT * FROM popes</div>
  </div>

  <div class="tool-card">
    <div class="tool-name"><code>search_texts(...)</code></div>
    <div class="tool-desc">Filter texts by pope, section, year, or regex.</div>
    <div class="wraps">database_helpers.<br>fetch_rows_by_regexp</div>
  </div>

  <div class="tool-card">
    <div class="tool-name"><code>find_biblical_citations()</code></div>
    <div class="tool-desc">Extract Bible refs from matching texts.</div>
    <div class="wraps">search.<br>search_biblical_citations_db</div>
  </div>

  <div class="tool-card">
    <div class="tool-name"><code>get_speech(url)</code></div>
    <div class="tool-desc">Fetch a single speech by URL.</div>
    <div class="wraps">SELECT ... FROM texts<br>WHERE url = ?</div>
  </div>

  <div class="tool-card">
    <div class="tool-name"><code>summarize_pope(name)</code></div>
    <div class="tool-desc">Cleaned per-pope corpus summary.</div>
    <div class="wraps">cleaning_pipeline.<br>get_clean_speech_metadata</div>
  </div>

</div>
```

---

## What it *might* feel like

::: {.callout-warning}
**Thought experiment, not a demo.** Nothing below is built. Numbers are made up.
:::

::: {.terminal}
```text
$ python -m dc26_vatican_explorer.agent   # imagined CLI

> Which 5 Bible verses does Francis quote most often in his
  homilies, and how does that compare to Benedict XVI?

[agent] planning...
[tool]  search_texts(pope="Francis", section="homilies")
        -> ~1,284 rows
[tool]  find_biblical_citations(pattern=DEFAULT)
        -> ~4,917 citations extracted
[tool]  search_texts(pope="Benedict XVI", section="homilies")
        -> ~962 rows
[tool]  find_biblical_citations(pattern=DEFAULT)
        -> ~3,201 citations extracted
[agent] aggregating...

Top 5 verses cited by Francis (homilies):
  1. Mt 25:40   -- 38 times
  2. Lk 15:20   -- 31 times
  3. Jn 13:34   -- 27 times
  4. 1 Jn 4:8   -- 22 times
  5. Mt 5:3     -- 19 times

Compared to Benedict XVI:
  - Francis cites Mt 25 (works of mercy) ~3x more often per homily.
  - Benedict cites Jn 1 and Rom 8 more than Francis.
  - Both share 1 Jn 4:8 ("God is love") in their top 10.

Sources: 47 homilies (URLs available via get_speech).
```
:::


---

::: {.section-divider}
[04]{.sec-num}
[Discussion]{.sec-title}
:::

---

## If we say yes: possible roadmap

**Milestones (for discussion)**

- **M1.** Tool wrappers + unit tests (mirror `tests/test_database_helpers.py`)
- **M2.** LLM loop + CLI entry point
- **M3.** Evaluation set: 20 gold Q/A pairs, regression-tested
- **M4.** *Optional* semantic search via embeddings (`sqlite-vec`)

**Risks to watch**

- Hallucinated SQL or citations → mitigate with typed tools + grounded answers
- Cost & latency → cache tool results, cap loop iterations
- Evaluation → build the gold set *before* we tune prompts

---

## Open questions for the group

::: {.incremental}
1. **Is this the right next feature?** Or should effort go into `pope_comparison/` instead?
2. **Which LLM provider?** Cloud (Claude/OpenAI) vs. local (Ollama): cost, privacy, reproducibility tradeoffs.
3. **Framework: PydanticAI, raw SDK, or LangChain?** How much abstraction do we want?
4. **Who is the user?** Us, other researchers, or a public-facing demo? Changes UX *and* security.
5. **How do we evaluate it?** What does "good enough" look like for a humanities corpus?
:::

---

## Thank you {background-gradient-radial="#fdf6e3, #e8d9a8"}

**Discussion time.**

- Repo: `dc26-vatican-explorer`, branch `agent`
- This is a proposal; nothing here is built yet
- Goal of this meeting: agree (or not) on the next concrete step
